fields.description:
# Stage-1 BM25F candidate generation over digests + graph-exclusion filter

## Why
Stage 1 narrows a few hundred tickets to ~20 candidates cheaply and deterministically, no LLM/embeddings, and must NOT surface a ticket's own already-related work (parents/children/siblings/linked) ‚Äî the top source of "related-but-distinct" false flags. DERISKED (epic experiment comment): digest-BM25F recall@20 = 1.0 on 8 paraphrase pairs, STABLE 60‚Üí200 corpus; single-query latency 0.69 ms at 800 digests (~14√ó under budget) ‚Äî so brute-force is correct and no ANN/vector-DB is needed.

## What
- Brute-force BM25F "More Like This" over `ticket_digest` records (S2). Bespoke ~40 LOC: `rank-bm25` provides only UNWEIGHTED BM25, but we need per-field weighting; at n<1000 bespoke is trivial and avoids a dependency (the recall+latency spikes validate this choice). Field weights are a module-level constant `_FIELD_WEIGHTS` in `retrieve.py` (`problem_keywords` 3.0, `component_or_area` 3.0, `key_entities` 1.0, `propositions` 1.0) ‚Äî a stable algorithmic choice, deliberately NOT a config knob (a dict-typed config value has no `from_env` resolution helper, and BM25F weights do not need per-invocation tuning; if they ever do, that is a separate `_llm_json` helper story). Tokenize lowercased on non-alphanumeric, drop stopwords + corpus-boilerplate via `overlap_max_doc_freq`. Returns a typed `OverlapCandidate` `[{ticket_id, score, matched_terms}]` (the interface S7 consumes ‚Äî stated so S7 isn't blind to it). Query side = the query ticket's freshly-extracted digest, passed by the caller.
- **Graph exclusion** BEFORE scoring: new helper `src/rebar/llm/overlap/graph.py::related_ticket_ids(ticket_id, tracker) -> set[str]` that loads states ONCE via `_load_all_states(tracker)` (the same helper `descendants.py` uses) and derives all four relation sets from that one dict: descendants (BFS on `parent_id`, or by calling `descendants.list_descendants(ticket_id, tracker)` and flattening its type-bucketed id lists ‚Äî NOT by sharing its internal snapshot, which it does not expose), ancestors (walk `parent_id` up), siblings (same `parent_id`; a root ticket with no parent has NO siblings ‚Äî handled explicitly), and linked tickets in BOTH directions (scan all tickets' `deps` for any edge whose source OR target is `ticket_id` ‚Äî an incoming `blocks`/`depends_on` from another ticket is not in this ticket's own `deps`, so outgoing-only would miss it). It does NOT refactor `next_batch.py`. At n<1000 one state load is sub-ms (latency spike).
- Cold/stale: corpus tickets whose digest is absent or stale (S2 freshness: content-hash/model/version mismatch ‚Äî not an age threshold) are SKIPPED; the skip-count is logged so a cold run is distinguishable from a healthy one.
- Overlap floor: a `min_should_match` fraction (default 0.15 of query terms) below which a candidate is not returned; `retrieve()` returns `[]` when nothing clears the floor.
- Errors: any graph-read/scoring exception is caught, logged, returns `[]` (never blocks review_plan).

## Config (LLMConfig, not VerifyConfig) ‚Äî with explicit `from_env` wiring
Three SCALAR tunables on `LLMConfig` (`src/rebar/llm/config.py`, `[tool.rebar.llm]`,
`REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_*`); field weights are a constant (see What), NOT config:
- `overlap_k: int = 20`
- `overlap_max_doc_freq: float = 0.5` (ignore terms appearing in >50% of digests)
- `overlap_min_should_match: float = 0.15`

**`LLMConfig.from_env()` MUST be edited** (it explicitly constructs `LLMConfig(...)` with
every field read via a helper ‚Äî a new dataclass field not read there stays at its default and
is silently un-tunable). Add module constants `DEFAULT_OVERLAP_K = 20`,
`DEFAULT_OVERLAP_MAX_DOC_FREQ = 0.5`, `DEFAULT_OVERLAP_MIN_SHOULD_MATCH = 0.15`; add the three
dataclass fields; and in `from_env`'s `return cls(...)` add:
- `overlap_k=_llm_int(table, cli, "REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_K", "overlap_k", DEFAULT_OVERLAP_K)`
- `overlap_max_doc_freq=_llm_float(table, cli, "REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_MAX_DOC_FREQ", "overlap_max_doc_freq", DEFAULT_OVERLAP_MAX_DOC_FREQ)`
- `overlap_min_should_match=_llm_float(table, cli, "REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_MIN_SHOULD_MATCH", "overlap_min_should_match", DEFAULT_OVERLAP_MIN_SHOULD_MATCH)`

`_llm_int` exists (`config.py:388`); `_llm_float` does NOT ‚Äî add it in `config.py` mirroring
`_llm_int` (env > `rebar -c llm.*` > `[tool.rebar.llm]` file > default, parsing via `float()`
with a clean fallback on ValueError). This is the only non-scalar-free helper needed (no dict
keys remain). `_config_schema.py` reserves the `llm.*` layer, so these are NOT on `VerifyConfig`.

## Dependencies
S1 (`ticket_digest` schema/fields), S2 (digest store + freshness). Digest fields are DEFINED in S1.

## Scope
- New `src/rebar/llm/overlap/retrieve.py` (BM25F + `OverlapCandidate` + `_FIELD_WEIGHTS` constant) and `src/rebar/llm/overlap/graph.py` (`related_ticket_ids`).
- `src/rebar/llm/config.py`: add `_llm_float` helper, the three `DEFAULT_OVERLAP_*` constants, the three `LLMConfig` fields, and the three `from_env()` reads (enumerated above).
- OUT: judge (S6), wiring (S7). Interface for S7: `retrieve(query_digest, corpus, exclude:set[str]) -> list[OverlapCandidate]`.

## Testing
- `tests/unit/test_overlap_retrieve.py`: `test_topk` (order); `test_perf` (<10 ms at 300 digests); `test_graph_exclusion` (parents/children/siblings/linked dropped; root-ticket-no-siblings edge); `test_cold_skip` (absent/stale skipped + count logged); `test_boilerplate` (shared boilerplate ‚âÝ match); `test_floor_empty` (nothing clears min_should_match ‚Üí `[]`); `test_error_empty` (graph error ‚Üí `[]`, no raise); `test_config` ‚Äî sets `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_K`, `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_MAX_DOC_FREQ`, `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_MIN_SHOULD_MATCH` in the env, calls `LLMConfig.from_env()`, and asserts the three fields resolve to the env values (proving `from_env` wiring, not just dataclass defaults); `test_field_weights_constant` (retrieve uses the `_FIELD_WEIGHTS` constant).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Returns top-K (default 20) `OverlapCandidate`s by field-weighted BM25F, <10 ms at ~300 digests; `pytest ...::{test_topk,test_perf}`.
- [ ] Candidates EXCLUDE the query's parents/children/siblings/linked via `related_ticket_ids(ticket_id, tracker)` (reusing `descendants.list_descendants`); root-with-no-parent has no siblings; `::test_graph_exclusion`.
- [ ] Absent/stale digests skipped, skip-count logged; `::test_cold_skip`. Boilerplate doesn't drive matches; `::test_boilerplate`.
- [ ] `min_should_match` floor returns `[]` when unmet; a graph/scoring error returns `[]` (never blocks); `::{test_floor_empty,test_error_empty}`.
- [ ] `overlap_k`, `overlap_max_doc_freq`, `overlap_min_should_match` are read from the env by `LLMConfig.from_env()` (explicit wiring, `_llm_float` added for the floats); field weights are the `_FIELD_WEIGHTS` constant, not config; `::test_config`, `::test_field_weights_constant`.
